item.title: Chains in episodes of autocratization (Preparing for Submission)
item.description: Are there patterns in the sequences of institutional change when democracies autocratize? If so, are such patterns distinct for democracies that transition into authoritarianism vs. those that avert democratic breakdown? And do such orders vary by the underlying conditions of autocratizing countries? The Episodes of Regime Transformation (ERT) dataset provides a global sample of all 69 autocratization episodes between 1900-2021. Using this data and pairwise domination analysis, we describe the general order of reforms in 31 variables which make up different types of accountability mechanisms constraining the government. The paper empirically demonstrates how different aspects of accountability decline during episodes of autocratization in democracies distinguishing between cases in which democracy ultimately breaks down or those in which breakdown is averted. Our findings suggest that the general institutional decay starts with horizontal accountability, followed by the decline in diagonal accountability, and finally, vertical accountability. This pattern becomes more apparent in the context of low democratic stock and the third wave of autocratization. The study is of high relevance not only for improving academic knowledge but also for policymakers and practitioners seeking to counteract further autocratization.
